content.claim: Total fertility rate in Hungary fell from 1.51 (2023) to 1.38 (2024) to 1.31 (2025); natural population decrease exceeded 52,000 in 2025; population fell to 9,489,000 by 1 January 2026, all representing accelerating decline, not stabilization.
content.context: Demographic decline through 2040 will force school-network consolidation and closure of small selective tracks independent of policy choice, but government's simultaneous use of score-threshold levers is accelerating selective-track contraction beyond what demographic decline alone would cause, concentrating remaining grammar-school slots among already-advantaged urban populations.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
